extra.reason: Storage design approved 2026-05-26. Three calls made in review session: (A) ServiceAccount soft-delete pinned as symmetric to Human; (B) re-bind-prohibition stands; (C) dropped 'no users registry' non-goal and added new architectural constraint #8 requiring cross-domain access through the users.Store interface (no direct SQL coupling from outside the identity-storage package). Doc on branch spgr-rjrt-identity-designs.

Authn design approved 2026-05-26. Three calls in review: (A) JIT-on-by-default stands; single-tenant is the common case and the config switch lets stricter operators flip it. (B) bootstrap user reframed as a system 'admin' identity (not seeded from OS username) to eliminate the bootstrap-vs-JIT collision footgun; this is a Bootstrap & UX doc change applied separately, no Authn body change needed. (C) process-local rate limit stands; shared rate-limit deferred.

Superseded 2026-05-26. During Self-Service Authz review, the question 'do we have a policy engine?' surfaced — answer was no, but we should. The skip-and-defer + handler-checks + runtime-registry mechanism this design specified is being replaced by a policy engine (Cedar). New bead filed for the adoption design; the existing Self-Service Authz doc will be deleted when the Cedar design lands.

Bootstrap & UX design approved 2026-05-26. Four calls: (1) Bootstrap user reframed as system identity with display_name literal 'admin' (not OS-user-seeded); eliminates JIT collision class. (2) Bootstrap admin persists indefinitely as recovery identity; force-flag-protected from accident; no auto-cleanup. (3) Cedar adoption acknowledged in Sequencing: bootstrap-affecting operations gated by policy not handler-side force flags. (4) Multi-server-URL credentials file moved from deferred non-goal to first-class design property; exact schema (URL-keyed vs profiles) deferred to impl. Doc updated on branch spgr-rjrt-identity-designs.

Cedar adoption design approved 2026-05-26. Four calls in review: (A) custom policies permissive by default — operators can ship policies via configured directories; hosted/multi-tenant configures tighter. (B) Stable action names decoupled from RPC method names; resolver maps (service, method) → action; survives proto refactors. (Wrapper) Cedar wrapped behind auth.PolicyEngine interface; only the wrapper imports cedar-go. (Source) PolicySource interface introduced for composable policy origins; built-in + filesystem ship initially; DB/URL sources land as follow-ups without engine changes. Doc on branch spgr-rjrt-identity-designs.
